abstract: Multi-query attention (MQA) significantly accelerates transformer decoder inference by utilizing only a single key-value head. However, MQA often results in quality degradation and typically requires training a separate model from scratch, which is computationally expensive. In this work, we first present a practical recipe for "uptraining" existing multi-head language model checkpoints into MQA models using only 5% of the original pre-training compute. Second, we introduce grouped-query attention (GQA), a generalized approach that uses an intermediate number of key-value heads to balance efficiency and capacity. Our results demonstrate that uptrained GQA models achieve performance levels nearly identical to standard multi-head attention while maintaining inference speeds comparable to MQA.
